channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Solvating magnesium polysulfides enables low–barrier speciation for magnesium sulfur batteries
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70598-7

Magnesium–sulfur batteries promise high energy density but face slow kinetics. Here, authors tailor electrolyte solvation to weaken magnesium-polysulfide binding, enabling improved cycling both coin and pouch cells.
item.dc:title: Solvating magnesium polysulfides enables low–barrier speciation for magnesium sulfur batteries
item.dc:creator: Jiayi Li
Wanyu Zhao
Keying Guo
Shang Shi
Yuegang Zhang
Xiaowei Yang

item.title: Wafer-scale manufacturing of ultra-broadband, high-power erbium-doped integrated lasers
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69787-1

Erbium-based fiber lasers are widely used but hard to integrate on chips at an industrial scale. Here, authors demonstrate the first wafer-scale erbium-doped silicon-nitride waveguide lasers with broad C+L-band tunability, high output power, and fiber laser-class coherence.
item.dc:title: Wafer-scale manufacturing of ultra-broadband, high-power erbium-doped integrated lasers
item.dc:creator: Xinru Ji
Xuan Yang
Yang Liu
Zheru Qiu
Grigory Lihachev
Simone Bianconi
Jiale Sun
Andrey Voloshin
Taegon Kim
Joseph C. Olson
Tobias J. Kippenberg

item.title: High-speed Si-Ge avalanche photodiode with a gain-bandwidth product of 7564 GHz
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70461-9

The authors showcase a lateral Si–Ge avalanche photodiode in the O-band. With low dark current, high gain–bandwidth product of 7564 GHz, it supports 100 Gbps NRZ and 200 Gbps PAM4 signals for high-speed optical communication applications.
item.dc:title: High-speed Si-Ge avalanche photodiode with a gain-bandwidth product of 7564 GHz
item.dc:creator: Jintao Xue
Chao Cheng
Shenlei Bao
Qian Liu
Wenfu Zhang
Binhao Wang
